chunks.text: Dan Garcia's research group GameCrafters featured in PBS Newshour article - EECS at Berkeley Prof. Dan Garcia was interviewed in a PBS Newshour article titled “ This Pi Day, use math to beat your friends at classic toy games ”. To celebrate Pi Day, mathematicians and physicists explored three classic toys, hula hoops, yo-yo’s and Connect Four on ways to be the most successful with each toy. To count how many possible ways a real game of Connect Four could end, Prof. Garcia’s research group called GamesCrafters created a program to simulate all the moves. GamesCrafters “strongly solved” the game and found 4,531,985,219,092, or 4.53 trillion combinations to Connect Four are possible. Categories Faculty Research Students View Open Faculty Positions
